title: Will there be a recession in 2026?
subtitle: Starts
resolution_source: If there are two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth in 2025 or 2026, according to the Bureau of Economic Analysis, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times?
subtitle: Exactly 0 cuts
resolution_source: If the Fed cuts 0 times starting Jan 1, 2026 and before 2027, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will the Fed cut rates 2 times?
subtitle: Exactly 2 cuts
resolution_source: If the Fed cuts 2 times starting Jan 1, 2026 and before 2027, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will the Fed cut rates 1 times?
subtitle: Exactly 1 cut
resolution_source: If the Fed cuts 1 times starting Jan 1, 2026 and before 2027, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will **real GDP** increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
subtitle: Above 2.0%
resolution_source: If real GDP (as measured by the BEA’s seasonally adjusted and annualized Advance Estimate) increases by more than 2.0, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will **real GDP** increase by more than 1.5% in Q1 2026?
subtitle: Above 1.5%
resolution_source: If real GDP (as measured by the BEA’s seasonally adjusted and annualized Advance Estimate) increases by more than 1.5, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027?
subtitle: Cuts
resolution_source: If the Federal Reserve cuts its target federal funds rate range at least once between February 26, 2026 and December 31, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.

title: Will **real GDP** increase by more than 2.5% in Q1 2026?
subtitle: Above 2.5%
resolution_source: If real GDP (as measured by the BEA’s seasonally adjusted and annualized Advance Estimate) increases by more than 2.5, then the market resolves to Yes.
